events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: residential fixture installation, staged, observed field condition: missing support independent of ceiling grid. Need choose correct tool/material/replacement approach and explain why observed choice wrong. Reference NEC 410. Need decision fail because non-compliant. Need risk? Missing support independent of ceiling grid likely for recessed luminaire or fixture supported by ceiling grid? NEC 410.36 means luminaires must be supported independently of ceiling grid? Actually NEC 410.36(B): "Means of Support. Luminaires and lampholders shall be supported independently of the outlet box unless the outlet box is listed and marked on the interior of the box to indicate the maximum weight the box shall be permitted to support." For suspended ceilings, 410.36(C): "Suspended Ceilings. Framing members of suspended ceiling systems used to support luminaires shall be securely fastened to each other and shall be securely fastened to the building structure at appropriate intervals. Luminaires shall be securely fastened to the ceiling framing member by mechanical means such as bolts, screws, or rivets. Listed clips identified for use with the type of ceiling framing member(s) and luminaire(s) shall be permitted." Also 410.36(B) exception? Need know exact. Missing support independent of ceiling grid means fixture cannot rely solely on ceiling grid; needs independent support wires/anchors to building structure. So correct approach: install approved support wires, rods, or independent mounting brackets anchored to building structure per NEC 410.36. Observed choice wrong because fixture supported by ceiling grid alone is prohibited; grid not designed to support luminaire weight and can sag/fall. Component condition installed-defective? Work staged, not installed. But observed field condition missing support independent of ceiling grid maybe existing fixture? The categories include installed-defective. Since condition is missing support, component condition installed-defective? Could be non-compliant. We can include installed-defective and non-compliant. Need percent_complete 0 (staged). value 0. confidence 1.0. event_time 0, rate 0, sound_source empty, order empty, workable empty. Findings: luminaire/fixture lacks independent support from building structure; intended support relies on suspended ceiling grid only. Actions: install independent support wires/rods/brackets anchored to structure per manufacturer and NEC 410.36; verify box/support rating for fixture weight; inspect before energizing. Rationale: NEC 410.36 requires luminaires be supported independently of outlet box or ceiling grid; suspended ceiling grid is not a structural support; missing independent support creates falling hazard and code violation. References: NEC 410.36(B), 410.36(C). Need ensure JSON valid.

  "findings": [
    "Fixture is not provided with support independent of the suspended ceiling grid.",
    "Reliance on the ceiling grid alone to carry the luminaire violates NEC support requirements and creates a falling/impact hazard."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Install independent support wires, rods, or approved brackets anchored to the building structure for the fixture.",
    "Verify the outlet box/support is listed and rated for the fixture weight per manufacturer instructions.",
    "Do not energize or cover the work until independent support is verified."
  ],
  "rationale": "NEC 410.36 requires luminaires to be supported independently of the outlet box or by a listed box rated for the load, and suspended ceiling grid members are not an acceptable sole support for a luminaire. The missing independent support is a code violation and a safety hazard, so the installation must be failed at this staged point.",
  "references": [
    "NEC 410.36(B)",
    "NEC 410.36(C)"
  ]
}
